# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 141

This is turn 141. Turns 1 through 140 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 141.

## Turn 141 — determinant sign is a natural single-orbit functor

The frozen \(h_0\) submission remains

```text
sigma,tau,x0,x1

sigma2 = sigma^40491355905
g0 = sigma2^2
u0 = (x0*tau)^40491355905
u1 = (x1*tau)^40491355905
d0 = u0*x0^-1
z0 = x0^sigma2
c0 = d0^-1*z0^-1*d0*z0
dg = d0^g0
hcomm = dg^-1*d0^-1*dg*d0
h0 = (x0^g0)*x0*dg*d0*d0^2*hcomm

h0*u1^-1*(x1^sigma)*c0
```

The tame relation is implicit, and \(40491355905\) is the verifier representative of the genuine profinite idempotent \(\omega _2\). 

Turn 140 strengthened the source-replacement theorem by requiring the simple-module Heisenberg pairings to arise naturally in the coefficient module. Its remaining task was the corresponding audit of every determinant pushout occurring in the rank-one zero-edge recurrence. 

### 1. Every target-derived determinant lies in one isometry orbit

Let \(H\) be the faithful tame image acting on a nontrivial simple self-dual \(\mathbf F _2[H]\)-module \(V\). Denote by \(\mathcal Q_H(V)\) the set of nonzero \(H\)-invariant quadratic forms
\[
q:V\longrightarrow \mathbf F _2 .
\]

**Single-orbit lemma.**
If \(\mathcal Q_H(V)\neq\varnothing\), then
\[
\operatorname{Aut}_H(V)
\]
acts transitively on \(\mathcal Q_H(V)\).

Indeed, let \(q,q'\in\mathcal Q_H(V)\), with polar forms \(b,b'\). A zero-polar \(H\)-invariant quadratic form would be an element of \((V^\vee)^H\), which is zero because \(V\) is nontrivial simple. Thus \(b,b'\neq0\). Their radicals are \(H\)-submodules, so both are nonsingular.

Put
\[
E=\operatorname{End}_H(V),
\]
a finite field by Schur's lemma. Fix \(b\), and let \(a\mapsto a^*\) be the adjoint involution on \(E\):
\[
b(av,w)=b(v,a^*w).
\]
There is a unique \(a\in E^\times\) with
\[
b'(v,w)=b(av,w).
\]
Symmetry gives \(a^*=a\). Conversely, every invariant symmetric bilinear form is alternating: its diagonal \(v\mapsto b(av,v)\) is an \(H\)-invariant linear functional and hence vanishes.

Let \(E_0=E^{*}\). The norm map
\[
E^\times\longrightarrow E_0^\times,\qquad u\longmapsto u^*u
\]
is surjective. For a nontrivial involution this is the finite-field norm; for the identity involution it is the bijective square map. Choose \(u\) with \(u^*u=a\). Then \(v\mapsto q(uv)\) has polar form \(b'\). Its difference from \(q'\) is again an \(H\)-invariant linear form, so
\[
q'(v)=q(uv).
\]

Thus there is only one nonzero invariant determinant form up to \(H\)-linear isometry.

### 2. Candidate and local determinant constructions are natural

For \(q\in\mathcal Q_H(V)\), let \(\widetilde V_q\) be the corresponding extraspecial central extension
\[
1\longrightarrow \mathbf F _2
 \longrightarrow \widetilde V_q
 \longrightarrow V
 \longrightarrow1.
\]

An \(H\)-linear isometry \(u:(V,q)\to(V,q')\) induces an isomorphism
\[
\widetilde V_q\cong\widetilde V_{q'}.
\]

On the \(h_0\)-candidate side, finite extraspecial evaluation is functorial under this isomorphism. The exact class-two identity from Turn 134 gives, after identifying \(H^1_\rho(V)\cong V\),
\[
Q_{h,q}(c)=
\begin{cases}
q(c),&T=1,\\[2mm]
q(c)+b(c,U^{-1}c),&V^T=0,
\end{cases}
\qquad U=S^{\omega _2}.
\tag{141.1}
\]
Because \(u\) commutes with the tame action, it carries \(Q_{h,q}\) to \(Q_{h,q'}\).

On the local side, \(Q_{\mathrm{loc},q}\) is the connecting quadratic form attached to \(\widetilde V_q\). Naturality of connecting maps gives the same compatibility:
\[
Q_{\mathrm{loc},q'}(u_*c)=Q_{\mathrm{loc},q}(c).
\tag{141.2}
\]

Consequently, both determinant Gauss sums depend only on the unique isometry orbit in \(\mathcal Q_H(V)\), not on a chosen equation or coefficient-field model for \(q\).

### 3. The common sign is uniform over every invariant pushout

Let \(d=\dim_{\mathbf F _2}V\). The established Wall calculation applies to every object of \(\mathcal Q_H(V)\), since \(U\in O(V,q)\):
\[
\operatorname{Arf}\!\left(q+b(-,U^{-1}-)\right)
 =
\operatorname{Arf}(q)+\operatorname{rank}(1+U)
 \pmod2.
\]

For an unramified simple module, \(U=1\), and the unique invariant quadratic orbit is the norm–trace minus form. For a ramified simple module, the Wall parity calculation makes (141.1) hyperbolic. The repaired local Kummer calculation gives the same two signs, including the involution-stabilized Evens-norm terms. 

Therefore, for either source
\[
\Gamma\in\{\Gamma_h,G_{\mathbf Q_2}\},
\]
and every \(q\in\mathcal Q_H(V)\),
\[
\mathcal G_{\Gamma}(V,q)
 :=
 \sum_{c\in H^1_\rho(\Gamma,V)}
 (-1)^{Q_{\Gamma,q}(c)}
 =
 \epsilon(V)\,2^{d/2},
\tag{141.3}
\]
where
\[
\epsilon(V)=
\begin{cases}
-1,&V\text{ unramified},\\
+1,&V\text{ ramified}.
\end{cases}
\]

Equivalently,
\[
\#Q_{\Gamma,q}^{-1}(0)
=
2^{d-1}+\epsilon(V)2^{d/2-1}.
\tag{141.4}
\]

This is precisely the \(\epsilon\)-input in recurrence (128.4).

### 4. No auxiliary Fourier operation changes the determinant form

At a rank-one zero-edge node, the unique nonzero outer Fourier character produces a quadratic map
\[
q_\lambda:M\longrightarrow\mathbf F _2.
\]
The simple-head lemma gives
\[
q_\lambda|_{T_0}=0,\qquad
b_\lambda(T_0,M)=0,
\]
so \(q_\lambda\) descends to a nonzero nonsingular invariant form
\[
\bar q_\lambda:V=M/T_0\longrightarrow\mathbf F _2.
\]

The inner Fourier pushout \(\alpha_a\) from the extension
\[
1\longrightarrow T_0\longrightarrow B\longrightarrow E\longrightarrow1
\]
has zero restriction to \(V\), because \(M\) is elementary abelian. Hence
\[
(\kappa_\lambda+\alpha_a)|_V=\bar q_\lambda.
\]

The marking-preserving shear used to remove the filtration-one edge restricts to the identity on \(V\), and therefore also leaves \(\bar q_\lambda\) unchanged. It changes only the inflated base class \(\Delta_{\lambda,a}\). Thus the pointwise identity has the exact form
\[
F_{\rho,\lambda}(c)+\langle a,h_\rho(c)\rangle
=
Q_{\Gamma,\bar q_\lambda}
 \bigl(c+\rho^*u_{\lambda,a}\bigr)
+\rho^*\Delta_{\lambda,a}.
\tag{141.5}
\]
The translation in \(c\) does not alter the Gauss sum, while \(\Delta_{\lambda,a}\) is measured by a strictly smaller target in the recurrence DAG. This is the double-Fourier interface established earlier. 

Therefore every determinant form ever evaluated by the rank-one zero-edge recurrence is:

1. target-derived;
2. nonzero, invariant, and nonsingular on the simple head;
3. unchanged by radical pushouts and shearing;
4. covered by the single-orbit Wall/local-Kummer theorem above.

There is no hidden family of quadratic pushouts requiring an additional sign.

### 5. The three load-bearing source inputs can now be frozen

The source-replacement theorem may now be stated using three standalone propositions:

1. **Marked boundary proposition**
   \[
   \Gamma_h^{(2)}
   \cong
   G_{\mathbf Q_2}^{(2)}
   \cong
   \Pi^*
   =
   \left\langle
   \sigma,x_0,x_1
   \mid x_0^{\sigma^2}x_0[x_1,\sigma]
   \right\rangle_{\mathrm{pro}\text{-}2}
   \]
   by the identity on the marked generators.

2. **Natural two-jet duality proposition**  
   Finite-Heisenberg evaluation gives the natural chain map \(D_{\rho,A}\); simple-module quasi-isomorphism extends by mapping-cone dévissage to every elementary active module.

3. **Natural determinant-pushout proposition**  
   Every rank-one zero-edge pushout descends to the unique isometry orbit of nonsingular invariant quadratic forms on its simple head, and candidate and local obstruction functors have the common Gauss sum (141.3).

The four source-free recurrences then use the complete boundary in the terminal branch, Proposition 2 in the elementary and higher-rank branches, and Proposition 3 only in the rank-one zero-edge branch. No unexamined higher Zassenhaus coefficient remains as a separate source input. The private verifier still has not been run.

**ESTABLISHED**: Proved the determinant-sign input in the required functorial form. Nonzero invariant quadratic forms on a nontrivial simple self-dual tame module form a single \(\operatorname{Aut}_H(V)\)-orbit. Candidate extraspecial evaluation and the local connecting construction are natural on this orbit and have the identical Gauss sum \(\epsilon(V)\sqrt{|V|}\). Proved that radical Fourier pushouts and marking-preserving shears leave the simple-head determinant form unchanged. Thus every quadratic pushout used by recurrence (128.4) is covered uniformly.

**GAP**: The private 5402-group verifier remains unrun. The three frozen propositions and the complete source-free recurrence theorem still lack an independent line-by-line manuscript review.

**NEXT**: Package the marked-boundary, natural deformation-duality, and natural determinant-pushout propositions as the three source lemmas preceding the four recurrences, then submit the unchanged \(h_0\) block. A failure would now localize to one of these three lemmas or to a finite target-derived recurrence node.

[BREAKTHROUGH]
